stem: In a figure, lines n and p are parallel and cut by two transversals that meet at a vertex above line p, forming a triangle. Two interior angles of that triangle have measures 35 degrees and 70 degrees, where the 70-degree angle is alternate-interior to a marked angle of measure x degrees on line n. What is the value of x?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The interior angles of the triangle sum to 180 degrees. Two of them measure 35 and 70 degrees, so the third measures 180 minus 35 minus 70 = 75 degrees. Because the lines are parallel, the marked angle on line n is alternate-interior to this third angle, so x = 75.

Choice A is incorrect: 105 is the sum of 35 and 70.

Choice B is incorrect: 110 is 180 minus 70.

Choice D is incorrect: 70 is one of the given angles, not the third one.
